source: Hematide ESA study, Drug Chem Toxicol 2008; Pandher et al., 2012, Toxicol Pathol; ICH S6(R1)
description: Expected findings for erythropoiesis-stimulating agents (epoetin alfa/beta, darbepoetin). Primary toxicity is exaggerated pharmacology: polycythemia and its cardiovascular sequelae. Rat and cynomolgus are the primary species. Inherits Fc-mediated base findings from general_mab.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: HGB 2-5 g/dL above baseline; HCT >60% in rats at high dose
expected_findings.severity_threshold: HCT >65% or cardiovascular sequelae (thrombosis, hypertension) = adverse. Cardiovascular death = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: Primary exaggerated EPO pharmacology. Secondary sequelae from severe polycythemia cause deaths in rats at high dose. (Hematide ESA study, Drug Chem Toxicol 2008)

expected_findings.severity_threshold: HCT >60% = monitor. HCT >65% with cardiovascular sequelae = adverse.

expected_findings.rationale: Reticulocyte surge is the primary pharmacodynamic response; indicator of effective EPO activity.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Near-total erythroid replacement of marrow space (E:M ratio >5:1) = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Expected histological correlate of EPO-driven erythropoiesis.

expected_findings.species_note: Rat has significant splenic erythropoietic reserve; EMH expected at pharmacological doses.
expected_findings.rationale: Rat splenic erythropoiesis is physiologically prominent; EPO drives marked expansion. (Hematide ESA study)
